content: GitHub repository ZubairL/Simulation_Drive_thru. Project is a discrete-event simulation of a fast-food drive-thru using SimPy. Models three stages of drive-thru operation: ordering, payment, and pickup. Entities: customers. Resources: order stations, payment stations, pickup stations. Activities: ordering, payment, pickup. Simulation runs over a 120-minute period. Tests varying key parameters (number of stations, customer arrival rates) to find the most efficient configuration. Uses SimPy + Python + Matplotlib. Specific parameter details (arrival rates, service times, station counts) live inside the Jupyter notebook in the repo and were not directly accessible from README-only fetching.
provenance_note: GitHub README fetch of the ZubairL drive-thru SimPy repo. The README is structural but parameter-free; the real parameters live in .ipynb and .py files which the current Seeder cannot traverse. Representative of the 'GitHub README underdescribes' friction pattern.
labels: github, simpy, zubairL, teaching, drive-thru, python

content: Information aggregated from multiple search-engine snippets describing the INFORMS Transactions on Education case article 'Case Article—Chick-fil-A Drive-Through: Managing Congestion with Discrete Event Simulation' (2023). Explicitly a teaching case for DES coursework. Models Chick-fil-A drive-through congestion management using DES methodology. Full case content (parameters, exact scenarios, pedagogical structure) is not accessible via automated fetching — the article is behind a publisher bot-block. Content here was accumulated from search engine result pages that described the paper rather than from direct extraction.
provenance_note: The source URL points to the INFORMS paper but the paper itself is publisher-bot-blocked (403) and was never successfully fetched. All content here was aggregated from multiple search engine result snippets describing the case article. INFORMS Transactions on Education teaching cases are a distinct source type (premium pedagogical content with full parameters and exercise sets) that deserves its own handling in the Seeder's priority table. Flagged for human follow-up via institutional INFORMS subscription.
labels: informs, chick-fil-a, teaching-case, paywalled, second-party, snippet-aggregated

content: Research findings on hybrid drive-thru ordering systems aggregated from multiple search-engine snippets across several unreachable sources (primarily Tandfonline paywalled papers and ResearchGate abstract pages). Key findings: hybrid ordering systems combining traditional counter/window ordering with self-service kiosks show measurable efficiency gains. A 3:3 self-service kiosk-to-counter ratio was found most effective, reducing total average wait time by 28%. Segmented payment counters reduced average queue length by 77%. Tools used in the drive-thru DES literature include Witness, FlexSim, AnyLogic, and SimPy. Hybrid ordering is a recurring scenario in recent fast-food operations research.
provenance_note: Aggregated from multiple search engine snippets rather than any single source fetch. Contributing sources include Tandfonline (paywalled) and ResearchGate summaries. Because this content is accumulated from multiple sources, source_url is NULL rather than pointing to any single URL — this pattern (multi-source snippet aggregation) differs from the single-source snippet aggregation in context row 2 (IEEE paper or INFORMS case where one URL describes the aggregation target).
labels: hybrid-ordering, kiosk, tandfonline, researchgate, second-party, multi-source-aggregation
